# World Cup 2026 — Findings & Projections

*Generated by the engine in `C:\Users\sambo\Desktop\WorldCup2026`. All numbers
are model output from real data. The match model is now **backtested, tuned and
calibrated** on ~8,000 held-out internationals (see [`BACKTEST.md`](BACKTEST.md));
the 48-team field is the **official 2026 final draw** (made 5 Dec 2025, completed
by the March 2026 play-offs) in `models/field_2026.py`; and the knockout stage
now uses the **official R32 seeding bracket** (FIFA Annex C), so the simulated
title odds follow the real draw path rather than a random bracket
(`python run.py simulate 20000`).*

---

## 1. Variables tracked (everything publicly digitised that helps prediction)

| Category | Specific variables | Source |
|---|---|---|
| Match results | date, teams, score, tournament, venue, neutral flag — **49,378 matches since 1872** | martj42/international_results |
| Knockout tiebreaks | penalty shootout winners (677) | martj42 shootouts |
| Expected goals (**xG**) | shot-level xG → match xG per team | StatsBomb open data |
| Live state | upcoming fixtures, in-play & final scores | TheSportsDB |
| News inflow | headlines auto-tagged by team + flag (**injury / suspension / lineup / form / transfer / manager**) | BBC, Guardian, Sky, ESPN RSS |
| Derived ratings | **Elo**, **attack/defence strength**, home advantage, time-decayed form | computed |

## 2. Derived measures the engine computes
- **International Elo** — tournament-weighted K-factor, home advantage, goal-difference multiplier (49k matches replayed).
- **Dixon-Coles attack & defence** — Poisson goal-rate strengths, MLE-fit with exponential time decay + L2 shrinkage, low-score (tau) correction.
- **Ordered-logit draw model + Ensemble 1X2** — blended win/draw/loss + expected goals + most-likely scoreline, with a fitted calibration temperature.
- **Monte Carlo title odds** — 20,000 simulated tournaments over the official bracket.

Every parameter above was tuned against **held-out Ranked Probability Score** —
the engine now scores **RPS 0.167 / log-loss 0.857** over 2018+ (vs 0.227 for a
base-rate baseline) and is calibrated to within ~1% (`BACKTEST.md`).

## 5. Official group stage (12 groups × 4) — with advance-to-knockout odds

The **official final draw**. `Adv%` = probability of finishing top-2 **or** as one
of the 8 best third-placed teams (32 of 48 advance, so the bar is low). Teams
listed by Elo within each group; `Adv%` is driven by *who else is in the group*,
so it isn't always monotonic with Elo (e.g. Australia clears Türkiye in D,
Germany clears Ecuador in E).

```
GROUP A                  Elo   Adv    GROUP B                  Elo   Adv
1 Mexico                1921   82%    1 Switzerland           1929   94%
2 South Korea           1866   77%    2 Canada                1877   86%
3 Czech Republic        1769   67%    3 Bosnia & Herz.        1625   48%
4 South Africa          1649   46%    4 Qatar                 1570   39%

GROUP C                  Elo   Adv    GROUP D                  Elo   Adv
1 Brazil                2049   98%    1 Turkey                1923   67%
2 Morocco               1961   92%    2 Paraguay              1884   61%
3 Scotland              1800   54%    3 Australia             1880   77%
4 Haiti                 1705   17%    4 United States         1807   69%

GROUP E                  Elo   Adv    GROUP F                  Elo   Adv
1 Ecuador               1990   90%    1 Netherlands           1985   86%
2 Germany               1971   97%    2 Japan                 1967   82%
3 Ivory Coast           1775   79%    3 Sweden                1753   51%
4 Curaçao               1591    9%    4 Tunisia               1733   51%

GROUP G                  Elo   Adv    GROUP H                  Elo   Adv
1 Belgium               1935   94%    1 Spain                 2190   98%
2 Iran                  1878   81%    2 Uruguay               1958   89%
3 Egypt                 1799   68%    3 Saudi Arabia          1678   34%
4 New Zealand           1717   25%    4 Cape Verde            1660   34%

GROUP I                  Elo   Adv    GROUP J                  Elo   Adv
1 France                2114   94%    1 Argentina             2162   98%
2 Norway                1926   75%    2 Austria               1865   73%
3 Senegal               1906   77%    3 Algeria               1860   76%
4 Iraq                  1729   24%    4 Jordan                1763   21%

GROUP K                  Elo   Adv    GROUP L                  Elo   Adv
1 Colombia              2035   90%    1 England               2055   97%
2 Portugal              2020   93%    2 Croatia               1947   88%
3 Uzbekistan            1807   42%    3 Panama                1823   35%
4 DR Congo              1753   37%    4 Ghana                 1606   41%
```

---

## 6. Tournament structure (the 2026 funnel)

```
   48 TEAMS  ──►  12 GROUPS (A–L), round-robin
       │
       ▼
   ROUND OF 32   ◄── 12 group winners + 12 runners-up + 8 best 3rd-placed
       │              (32 of 48 advance)
       ▼
   ROUND OF 16  ── official FIFA bracket (Annex C seeding) ──┐
       │                                                      │ winners/runners
       ▼                                                      │ fixed by group
   QUARTER-FINALS ── 8 ──                                     │ letter; the 8
       │                                                      │ best thirds slot
       ▼                                                      │ in by the
   SEMI-FINALS ── 4 ──                                        │ published rules
       │                                                      │
       ▼                                                      │
   FINAL ── 2 ── ◄────────────────────────────────────────────┘
       │
       ▼
   🏆 CHAMPION 2026   →  model favourite: ARGENTINA (20.5%), then SPAIN (17%) & BRAZIL (14%)
```

New venues: 16 host cities across USA, Canada & Mexico. First 48-team,
104-match World Cup. Hosts (USA, Canada, Mexico) seeded into pot 1 by convention.

---

## 7. Headline reads
- **Three at the top now**: Argentina (20.5%) and Spain (17.1%) still lead, but the
  recency-corrected ratings (longer Dixon-Coles memory) lift **Brazil to a clear
  third (13.9%)** — ahead of England (8.0%) and France (7.5%).
- **The draw is lopsided.** With the real bracket wired in, **Argentina, Brazil,
  England and Portugal all sit in the same half**, while **Spain and France anchor
  the lighter half**. So two of {Argentina, Brazil, England} are eliminated before
  the final — and Spain's clearer path is part of why it holds 17% despite ranking
  second on raw Elo.
- **Chasing pack** (England, France, Portugal, Colombia): each 5–8%; live but not
  dominant.
- **Dark horses**: Colombia (5.2%) and Morocco (3.3%); Belgium (3.1%) and Germany
  (2.7%) lurk despite middling group draws.
- **Toughest group**: Group E — Ecuador (90%), Germany (97%) and Ivory Coast (80%)
  are all live, so a real side exits in the group; Curaçao (9%) is the field's
  longest shot. Group K pits Colombia and Portugal (two top-7 sides) against each
  other.
- **Accuracy, measured**: the engine isn't just plausible — it's scored. On 8,000
  held-out internationals it lands **RPS 0.167 / log-loss 0.857** and is calibrated
  to ~1% mean error (`BACKTEST.md`).

*Methodology: International Elo + Dixon-Coles Poisson goals model (time-decayed
MLE) + an ordered-logit Elo draw model, blended 40/60 with a fitted calibration
temperature; all parameters tuned on held-out Ranked Probability Score. Monte
Carlo (20,000 runs) over the **official** 2026 group draw and **official R32
seeding bracket**. Re-runs as live results + news flow in via
`python run.py refresh`.*
